tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Six-Year-Old's Chores Bloom Into Friendship and Handmade Love
stories.summary: Six-year-old Luke earned money doing chores for elderly neighbor Helen to buy a sewing machine, then hand-sewed her a Star Wars pillow to say 'I love her.'

stories.headline: Soft Glove Restores Touch: ALS Patient Holds Fork Again
stories.summary: Researchers develop a pneumatic exoskeleton glove that reads muscle signals, allowing a paralyzed patient to grasp objects for the first time in years.

stories.headline: Messi's Magic: Argentina Stuns Egypt With Three Goals in 11 Minutes
stories.summary: Down 2-0 and on the brink of elimination, Argentina orchestrated an improbable World Cup comeback, with Messi leading the charge in a moment that left fans in tears.

stories.headline: City's Spoonbills Soar: From Rare Visitors to 7,000 Thriving Neighbors
stories.summary: Black-faced spoonbills surged from 430 endangered birds in 1995 to 7,081 today, with 3,828 now thriving in Incheon's artificial wetlands.

stories.headline: Strangers Become Angels: Community Rallies Around Stranded Drivers
stories.summary: When a fatal collision trapped motorists for hours, Rick Bursey and Roseanne Martin from North West Brook stepped up with water and supplies.

stories.headline: Playing Through Grief: Dodgers Catcher Honors Lost Family With Debut
stories.summary: Dodgers catcher Eliezer Alfonzo played his debut hours after learning his teenage sister and stepmother died in Venezuelan earthquakes, finding strength to honor their memory on the field.

stories.headline: Donor's Gift Gives Veteran a Home After Losing Everything
stories.summary: Ron Hoover donated an RV camper and $1,000 to retired veteran Jeff Compson after a landfill fire destroyed his home.

stories.headline: Fisherman's Compassion Frees Endangered Pangolin Back to the Wild
stories.summary: A Palawan fisherman reported an endangered pangolin tangled in his net, leading to its safe rescue and release back into Palawan's protected forests.

stories.headline: Forest Guardians: White-Lipped Peccaries Return Home After Training
stories.summary: Biologist Guido Saborío led the release of 13 white-lipped peccaries back into Costa Rica's Piedras Blancas National Park after training them to enter corrals voluntarily.

stories.headline: Three Friends Turn Loose Change Into $155,000 for Community
stories.summary: Walter Wilson, 90, and Pete Petty, 76, led volunteers collecting bottles 'a dime at a time' to donate $155,000 to their community along the McKenzie River.

stories.headline: First Commercial Nuclear Satellite Powers Up for Deep-Space Missions
stories.summary: SpaceX launches BOHR, the first commercial satellite powered by safe tritium technology, opening new possibilities for exploring dark lunar craters and beyond.

stories.headline: Artificial Pancreas Rolls Out Across UK, Transforming Diabetes Care
stories.summary: The NHS's groundbreaking artificial pancreas technology is transforming type 1 diabetes care while narrowing healthcare gaps between wealthier and poorer communities.
